ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for the specific combination of grilled pork chop with Vietnamese egg meatloaf and rice. Mapped to closest parent 'pork dish' (FOODON_00002764) as the most defensible available anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for this composite Vietnamese-Singaporean hawker dish. A0FJP (Pig meat, cooked) is used as the closest defensible parent anchor covering the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific composite hawker dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker plate comprising approximately 1 grilled pork chop (80–100 g), one portion of Vietnamese egg meatloaf (60–80 g), and a serving of steamed white rice (150–170 g), totalling approximately 350 g.

nutrient_source.title: Grilled Pork Chop With Meatloaf — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'R1. Grilled Pork Chop With Meatloaf 猪排肉饼饭' (cr_id G00010801). The HPB entry reports values for the combined dish as served. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 (data not available) and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — White rice (steamed)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: The HPB nutrient record reports carbohydrate_g as 0.0 g/100g for the meat-and-meatloaf component only; however, the dish name '猪排肉饼饭' explicitly includes rice (饭). The GI of 72 is assigned based on the white rice component (steamed jasmine rice, GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database). The glycemic load per serving is calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100. Because the HPB nutrient data does not capture the rice carbohydrate in its per_100g figures, GL is reported as 0.0 against the HPB-sourced carbohydrate figure, with a caveat that the true GL for the full plate (including rice) is substantially higher. Consumers and clinicians should note that a 150 g rice portion contributes approximately 55 g available carbohydrate, yielding an estimated GL of ~40 for the rice component alone.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; soy sauce marinade and processed meatloaf seasoning are primary contributors; potassium at 47 mg/100 g provides no meaningful offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request reduced-sodium soy sauce, minimal added salt in both the pork chop marinade and meatloaf, and substitute white rice with additional steamed vegetables to reduce overall sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: The HPB nutrient record captures only the meat component (carbohydrate 0 g/100 g), but the dish includes white rice (饭), a high-GI carbohydrate (GI ~72) that significantly raises postprandial blood glucose; the meatloaf may also contain refined carbohydrate from binders.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should request a smaller rice portion or substitute with brown rice or cauliflower rice, verify total carbohydrate content with the vendor, and pair the dish with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 1050 mg/100 g poses a direct cardiovascular risk; pork chop and ground pork meatloaf contribute saturated fat and dietary cholesterol (89 mg/100 g); the processed meatloaf preparation adds further sodium and likely saturated fat not fully captured in the HPB data.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to very high sodium and likely saturated fat content; if consumed, choose a smaller portion, request grilling without added oil, and avoid high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce and fish sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork (red meat) is high in arachidonic acid, a precursor to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids; processed meatloaf adds further saturated fat and sodium; sodium at 1050 mg/100 g may worsen RA-related tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat frequency, request grilling without added oil to minimise saturated fat, and be aware that the high sodium content may exacerbate inflammatory tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Pork Chop with Vietnamese Egg Meatloaf and Rice (猪排肉饼饭; Vietnamese: Cơm Sườn Nướng Chả Trứng) is a popular Singaporean hawker dish combining two distinct protein preparations served over steamed white rice. The grilled pork chop is typically marinated in soy sauce, fish sauce, garlic, and lemongrass before being chargrilled to produce a caramelised, slightly smoky exterior with a juicy interior. The Vietnamese egg meatloaf (chả trứng hấp) is a steamed or pan-fried loaf made from ground pork, whole eggs, wood-ear mushrooms (black fungus), and mung bean vermicelli (glass noodles), bound together and cooked until firm with a savoury, eggy flavour and a dense, moist texture. The dish reflects the Vietnamese culinary influence present in Singapore's hawker culture. Per 100 g of the combined meat-and-meatloaf component (HPB data), the dish provides 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and 0 g carbohydrate — noting that the HPB figure does not include the rice component. Sodium is very high at 1050 mg/100 g, driven by soy sauce marinade and meatloaf seasoning, making this dish a concern for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The white rice component (not captured in the HPB per-100g figure) contributes a high glycaemic load. Clinically, this dish is high-concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to sodium, and moderate-concern for type 2 diabetes due to the rice component. It is not suitable for low-sodium diets. The dish is dairy-free and not halal due to pork content.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010801, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes. The dish is an exact match in the HPB database. Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown are not reported by HPB and are set to -1. The carbohydrate figure of 0 g/100 g reflects the HPB data scope (meat component only) and is documented with justification. GI is estimated from the white rice component using the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): carbohydrate_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g and sugar_g as 0.0 g/100 g. This reflects the HPB database entry scope, which covers the grilled pork chop and Vietnamese egg meatloaf components only and does not include the white rice (饭) served with the dish. The meat-and-meatloaf components are naturally very low in carbohydrate; the HPB-reported value of 0.0 is confirmed zero per the HPB Foodo database for this specific entry. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g, confirmed zero per HPB for the meat component. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, fat-soluble vitamins, B-vitamins, and moisture data for this composite hawker dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010801 as of 2026-04-16. GLYCEMIC INDEX NOTE: The HPB nutrient record captures only the meat-and-meatloaf component (carbohydrate 0 g/100 g). The dish name explicitly includes rice (饭/cơm). GI of 72 is assigned based on the white rice component (steamed jasmine rice) per the University of Sydney GI Database. The glycemic load per serving is reported as 0.0 against the HPB carbohydrate figure; the true GL for the full plate including rice is substantially higher (estimated GL ~40 for the rice portion alone). TRANS FAT NOTE: The input health_suitability section referenced 'Trans fat 0.1g/100g' for the rheumatoid arthritis caution; however, no trans fat value is reported in the HPB per_100g data. trans_fat_g has been set to -1 (not reported by HPB) rather than 0.1, as no approved source confirms this value. HALAL STATUS: This dish contains pork and is not halal. ONTOLOGY NOTE: foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type are set to 'closest_parent' as no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for this specific composite Singaporean-Vietnamese hawker dish; the mapped codes represent the closest defensible parent categories.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full RESULT JSON. Identified that carbohydrate_g = 0.0 required zero-macro justification (g4 rule). Identified that saturated_fat_g and other fatty acid fields not in HPB data required -1 sentinel with g3 documentation. Identified that GI needed analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for the rice component. Added all mandatory multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, Vietnamese) for Singaporean dish with Vietnamese influence. Added Type 2 Diabetes health context with correct machine key. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback. Corrected foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type to 'broader' as per the allowed values. Added 'Com Suon Nuong Cha Trung' to the flat aliases array to ensure consistency with aliases_by_language.romanized. Confirmed zero-macro justification present in notes. Confirmed all -1 sentinel fields documented.

Iteration 3: Addressed validator feedback from iteration 2. Corrected foodon_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' and foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enum values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by g5_ontology_honesty. Updated ontology gap notes to reflect 'closest parent' terminology. Added ontology note to the notes field. All other sections preserved unchanged from iteration 2 as they passed all gates and scored 8/8.
